title: satureja leucantha
intro: Satureja leucantha, also known as white savory, is a flowering plant in the family Lamiaceae, native to the Mediterranean region and parts of Africa. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. Satureja leucantha has been used to aid digestion, relieve respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, and to treat skin conditions such as wounds and acne, thanks to its high content of essential oils. The herbal preparations of Satureja leucantha include teas, infusions, and essential oils, which can be used topically or internally to harness its health benefits.
